title: Agent Playground Recipe — ap.recipe/v0.2
description: Schema for Agent Playground recipe YAML files consumed by tools/run_recipe.py. See docs/RECIPE-SCHEMA.md for the canonical specification. v0.2 flips the reserved oneOf discriminator seam live (root now matches either v0_1 or v0_2 branch on apiVersion const) and adds two additive top-level blocks under v0_2: persistent: (daemon/gateway mode + health_check oneOf) and channels: (messaging platform wiring with channel_verified_cell, user_input_entry, channel_category enum). v0.1 / v0.1.1 recipes remain valid unchanged under the v0_1 branch.
$comment: Root now uses oneOf with two apiVersion-discriminated branches. v0_1 matches apiVersion=ap.recipe/v0.1 exactly (via const). v0_2 matches apiVersion=ap.recipe/v0.2 exactly (via const). Each branch carries additionalProperties:false — a v0.1 recipe with a persistent/channels block will NOT cross-validate against v0_1 (correct — must opt in via apiVersion bump). Pre-existing WR-01 note (error-path opacity under oneOf) is accepted as the cost of versioning; future plans can re-route to anyOf if user-facing error messages regress.

category.description: Authoritative smoke-level failure category. 9 live (PASS, ASSERT_FAIL, INVOKE_FAIL, BUILD_FAIL, PULL_FAIL, CLONE_FAIL, TIMEOUT, LINT_FAIL, INFRA_FAIL) + 2 reserved: STOCHASTIC (reserved — phase 15), SKIP (reserved — later UX phase). Used by smoke.verified_cells[*].category and smoke.known_incompatible_cells[*].category. v0.2 introduces a distinct channel_category enum (below) for channel-scoped verified_cells so BLOCKED_UPSTREAM does not leak into v0.1 semantic space.

channel_category.description: Channel-scoped category enum (v0.2). Superset of smoke `category` enum + BLOCKED_UPSTREAM, which flags cells where channel wiring proved correct but an upstream provider bug blocks the LLM round-trip (e.g. openclaw openrouter plugin silent-fail documented in channels.telegram.known_quirks).

health_check.description: Persistent-mode readiness probe. One-of discriminated on kind.

oneOf.description: Runner asserts `docker inspect <cid>` reports State.Running == true. Used by recipes whose daemon has no HTTP health endpoint (hermes, nullclaw).

port.description: TCP port inside the container exposing the health endpoint.

oneOf.description: Runner GETs http://<container>:<port><path> after ready_log_regex match; first 2xx is a pass. Per spike-11: picoclaw/nanobot use /health (not /ready, which returns 503 even when Telegram is connected); openclaw uses / on port 18789.

persistent_block.description: v0.2 persistent-mode declaration. Parallel to invoke: — invoke: is the one-shot smoke path, persistent: is the live-session path driven by channels:.

argv.description: Ordered argv passed after the container entrypoint. Same $MODEL / $VAR substitution rules as invoke.spec.argv minus $PROMPT.

ready_log_regex.description: Python regex matched against container stdout+stderr by the runner after docker run -d. First match signals ready.

graceful_shutdown_s.description: SIGTERM → docker wait timeout before docker rm -f. Range [0, 600] seconds. The value 0 is a sentinel meaning 'skip graceful shutdown and go straight to docker rm -f' — used by recipes whose daemon ignores SIGTERM (nanobot gateway per spike-07). Pair with sigterm_handled:false for documentation clarity.

entrypoint.description: Docker --entrypoint override for persistent mode. Same semantics as invoke.spec.entrypoint. Absent when the image entrypoint is already correct (hermes). 'sh' for recipes that heredoc-chain config + exec (picoclaw, nullclaw, nanobot, openclaw).

user_override.description: Docker --user override. Required when the image has ownership bugs that block the declared runtime.volumes[*] mount. nullclaw-only today ('root').

sigterm_handled.description: Documentation flag — true asserts the agent handles SIGTERM gracefully within graceful_shutdown_s. If absent, runner still applies the graceful timeout + force-remove fallback.

argv_note.description: Free-form explanation of why the argv is shaped the way it is. Lives at persistent.<top-level>, parallel to invoke.spec.argv_note.

lifecycle_note.description: Free-form operational notes: invocation examples, shutdown signals, startup wall times.

direct_interface_block.description: v0.2 direct_interface declaration (Phase 22b D-19..D-22). Top-level sibling of `persistent` + `channels`. Declares the recipe's primary programmatic invocation surface — a docker exec CLI invocation OR an HTTP POST to an in-container endpoint. Used by SC-03 Gate A (test/lib/agent_harness.py send-direct-and-read) to invoke the agent directly without involving the channel layer (Telegram bot-self impersonation is filtered by allowFrom; direct_interface bypasses the gateway entirely). Spike C 2026-04-19: outer `additionalProperties: false` was REMOVED — JSON Schema does not propagate oneOf-branch property declarations to the outer level, so an outer additionalProperties:false would reject `kind` and `spec` BEFORE oneOf branches validate. Each oneOf branch carries its own `additionalProperties: false` which is the actual closure mechanism.

argv_template.description: argv list passed after `docker exec <cid>`. {prompt} and {model} are substituted by the harness via Python str.format() before invocation. SUBSTITUTION IS LITERAL — no shell expansion (subprocess.run(shell=False)). For sh-chained recipes, use the explicit form ['sh', '-c', '<single-string-pipeline>'] (openclaw post-22b-07 example).

timeout_s.description: Wall-time budget for the docker exec. Range [1, 300] seconds. Default 60 if omitted by harness logic.

stdout_reply.description: true → reply text is captured from stdout (typical). false reserved for future stderr-only modes.

reply_extract_regex.description: Optional regex applied to stdout. If the regex matches AND has a named group `reply`, harness uses m.group('reply'); else uses m.group(0); else falls back to full stdout. Used to strip trailing session_id lines (hermes), strip JSON envelope wrappers (openclaw), etc.

exit_code_success.description: Expected exit code for a successful invocation. Defaults to 0; some agents emit non-zero in success states (none currently in catalog, reserved).

port.description: TCP port inside the container exposing the OpenAI-compatible chat completions endpoint.

path.description: URL path; must begin with '/'. Typical: /v1/chat/completions

header.description: HTTP header name carrying the credential. Typical: Authorization.

value_template.description: Template string. {api_key} is substituted with the harness --api-key value at invocation time. Typical: 'Bearer {api_key}'.

request_template.description: OpenAI-compatible request body template. The harness merges {prompt} into messages[].content at invocation time; other fields (model, temperature, etc.) flow through verbatim. additionalProperties:true to accommodate per-recipe model parameters.

response_jsonpath.description: Tiny JSONPath subset (must start with '$'). Resolves to the assistant's reply text in the JSON response. Typical: $.choices[0].message.content

timeout_s.description: Wall-time budget for the curl invocation. Range [1, 300] seconds. Default 60 if omitted.

notes.description: Free-form notes hoisted to the outer block (nullclaw + openclaw both carry a `notes:` field — Spike C 2026-04-19 verified the field is load-bearing in those recipes; declared here at the outer level so it applies to ALL oneOf branches without per-branch repetition).

event_source_fallback.description: v0.2 channel-scoped event source dispatcher (Phase 22b D-23). Declares the alternate observation path when docker logs do NOT carry per-message events for this recipe. Watcher_service _select_source() (api_server/src/api_server/services/watcher_service.py line 380-387) dispatches on `kind`. When this block is ABSENT entirely, the watcher defaults to docker_logs_stream (the typical case for hermes/picoclaw/nanobot whose docker logs DO carry events). Spike C 2026-04-19: outer `additionalProperties: false` REMOVED for the same reason as direct_interface_block (oneOf branch properties don't propagate); outer-level `notes` property hoisted to allow nullclaw + openclaw's load-bearing notes field. Inner branches retain `additionalProperties: false`.

spec.description: No required spec — docker_logs_stream uses Docker SDK logs(follow=True) directly. spec field reserved for future tuning (buffer size, etc.).

argv_template.description: argv passed to docker exec. {session_id} and {chat_id} are substituted at watcher invocation time.

session_id_template.description: Template producing the session_id from chat_id. {chat_id} is substituted. nullclaw example: 'agent:main:telegram:direct:{chat_id}'.

tail_file.description: Optional alternative observation surface — a file path the watcher can `docker exec tail -f`. Currently used by nullclaw as a 'something happened' signal even when the per-turn JSON poll is unreliable.

sessions_manifest.description: Container-path to the JSON file mapping chat_ids to session_ids. openclaw example: /home/node/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json

session_log_template.description: Container-path template for per-session JSONL files. {session_id} is substituted at watcher invocation time.

user_input_entry.description: v0.2 channel user-input declaration. Drives deploy-form field rendering. Used for both required_user_input[] and optional_user_input[]. `secret` is required by convention on required_user_input entries (runner treats missing secret as false) and usually omitted on optional_user_input entries — the schema keeps it optional to accommodate the hermes optional_user_input pattern where operational-only entries (TELEGRAM_HOME_CHANNEL) have no secret flag.

env.description: Env var name the agent reads.

secret.description: true → treat as BYOK secret: inject via --env-file, redact from logs, UI masks input. false (or absent) → operational config.

hint.description: User-facing short help for the deploy form.

kind.description: Typed hint for client-side input validation.

prefix_required.description: Runner auto-prepends this literal prefix to the value BEFORE injection into the file-transport heredoc. openclaw-only today ('tg:').

bot_username.description: The bot the cell was run against.

verdict.description: Channel verdict enum. Distinct from smoke.verified_cells[*].verdict ({PASS, FAIL}). PASS_WITH_FLAG (added Spike C 2026-04-19): semantic for nullclaw + openclaw recipes whose channel + LLM both work but a non-blocking quirk is documented (e.g., openclaw openrouter plugin upstream-blocked, anthropic-direct path PASSes; the `_WITH_FLAG` suffix signals the cell is functionally green but the operator should read the notes).

model.description: Model identifier when the cell exercised a specific LLM binding. Absent for hermes (auxiliary auto-detects).

provider.description: Which upstream LLM provider the cell used (openrouter | anthropic | openai | ...).

env_var.description: Which env var supplied the provider key (disambiguates multi-provider recipes).

boot_wall_s.description: Observed wall time from docker run -d to ready_log_regex match. Accepts integer (12), float (2.85), or YAML literal-string approximations (`~3`, `~6` — note YAML parses unquoted ~3 as the string '~3', not the number 3) for human-friendly recon notes.

first_reply_wall_s.description: Observed wall time from first user message to first agent reply. Same accepted shapes as boot_wall_s.

reply_sample.description: Verbatim or lightly redacted sample of the first reply.

spike_artifact.description: Optional path to a related spike artifact (e.g., .planning/phases/22b-agent-event-stream/22b-SPIKES/spike-01e-openclaw.md) for full evidence trail. Used by 4 of 5 v0.2 recipes (Spike C 2026-04-19: picoclaw + nullclaw + nanobot + openclaw).

channel_entry.description: v0.2 channel wiring entry. Keyed by channel id (telegram, discord, slack, ...) in channels_block.

config_transport.description: env → runner injects channel env vars via --env-file. file → runner writes a config file inside the container (typically via sh-heredoc in persistent.spec.argv).

required_user_input.description: Ordered list. Drives deploy-form field order.

optional_user_input.description: Optional extras; same shape as required_user_input.

ready_log_regex.description: Per-channel ready signal pattern. May differ from persistent.spec.ready_log_regex when one gateway hosts multiple channels.

response_routing.description: per_message_origin = reply lands in sender's chat. fixed_home_channel = reply always lands in a preconfigured chat.

multi_user_model.description: How the agent decides which users it will serve.

multi_account_supported.description: Defaults to false. true iff the recipe can run multiple bot accounts within one container.

supported.description: LLM provider IDs with end-to-end empirical PASS on this channel.

deferred.description: LLM provider IDs the recipe explicitly disallows until the upstream bug clears.

known_quirks.description: Channel-scoped caveats. DISTINCT shape from smoke.known_quirks ({quirk,impact}); the two are intentionally NOT unified.

approve_argv.description: docker exec argv the runner runs to approve a pairing code. $CODE is substituted with the operator-supplied value. Example: ['openclaw','pairing','approve','telegram','$CODE'].

event_log_regex.description: v0.2 channel-scoped log-line regex map (Phase 22b D-04). Keys: event kind names (reply_sent, reply_failed, agent_ready, agent_error from VALID_KINDS, plus optional inbound_message + response_ready for the hermes-style metric pipeline). Values: regex string (Python re syntax) OR null (signals 'this kind is NOT observable via docker logs for this recipe' — consumer must use event_source_fallback). additionalProperties allowed because some recipes add bespoke metric kinds (response_ready in hermes).

event_source_fallback.description: v0.2 channel-scoped event source dispatcher (Phase 22b D-23). Optional — defaults to docker_logs_stream when absent. REQUIRED in practice when ANY event_log_regex.<kind> is null (the watcher needs an alternative observation path for the null kinds; nullclaw + openclaw use this today).

channels_block.description: v0.2 channels block. Keys are channel ids (telegram, discord, slack, ...). Each value is a channel_entry.

name.description: Short slug used to tag the built image (ap-recipe-<name>) and as the default needle for response_contains_name.

display_name.description: Human-readable name shown in logs and future UI.

description.description: What the agent is and which invocation path this recipe covers.

ref.description: Git ref — SHA (preferred), tag, or branch name. Allowlisted charset defends against `git fetch` option-as-value injection (e.g. `--upload-pack=<cmd>`) by rejecting anything outside plain ref characters.

ref_note.description: Free-form explanation of what the ref points at and why.

upstream_version.description: The upstream's self-declared version at recon time. Documentation only.

dockerfile.description: Path to Dockerfile relative to clone root. Defaults to Dockerfile.

context.description: Path to build context relative to clone root. Defaults to '.'.

image.description: Full registry reference for image_pull mode.

observed.description: Documentation-only record of what the recipe author saw at recon time.

timeout_s.description: Max wall time for docker build (upstream_dockerfile mode) or docker pull (image_pull mode). Default 900 seconds when absent. Capped at 10800 (3h) — accommodates hermes + openclaw cold builds.

clone_timeout_s.description: Max wall time for git clone + checkout in upstream_dockerfile mode. Default 300 seconds when absent. Capped at 1800 (30min) — accommodates large repos.

annotations.description: Open extension point for recon fields, tool-specific metadata, and forward-compatible annotations. Reserved for future use per the OpenAPI x-* / Kubernetes annotations pattern.

provider.description: Which LLM gateway the recipe plumbs (e.g. openrouter, anthropic, openai).

api_key.description: Canonical env var name the agent reads (e.g. OPENROUTER_API_KEY).

api_key_fallback.description: Documentation only. An alternate env var name the agent's own code accepts internally.

base_url.description: Override for the provider base URL. null means agent's default is fine.

model.description: Default model. null means set per-call.

name.description: Identifier for logs and future orchestrator use.

host.description: Host path or the sentinel per_session_tmpdir.

ephemeral.description: Documentation flag — true means volume is thrown away at teardown.

owner_uid.description: UID the container runs as. Full Linux uid_t range (32-bit unsigned); values > 65535 typically indicate userns-remap.

notes.description: Free-form notes about what the agent writes here.

id.description: Short identifier, unique within the recipe.

reason.description: Evidence: file paths, line numbers, empirical observations.

annotations.description: Open extension point for recon fields, tool-specific metadata, and forward-compatible annotations. Reserved for future use per the OpenAPI x-* / Kubernetes annotations pattern.

mode.description: Only cli-passthrough is implemented in v0.1.

entrypoint.description: Value passed to docker run --entrypoint. Omit to use image's baked entrypoint.

argv.description: Ordered argv passed after the entrypoint. Runner substitutes $PROMPT and $MODEL.

argv_note.description: Free-form explanation of why the argv is shaped the way it is.

engine.description: Only awk is implemented in v0.1.

program.description: For engine awk, a valid awk program.

notes.description: What the filter does and why.

program.description: Awk program that prints the session ID on one line.

notes.description: How the resulting ID can be reused.

annotations.description: Open extension point for recon fields, tool-specific metadata, and forward-compatible annotations. Reserved for future use per the OpenAPI x-* / Kubernetes annotations pattern.

prompt.description: Default prompt sent to the agent.

needle.description: Literal substring to search for. Required for response_contains_string and response_not_contains.

regex.description: Python regex pattern. Required for response_regex.

case_insensitive.description: Default false. Applies to response_contains_name, response_contains_string, response_not_contains.

timeout_s.description: Max wall time for the container run. Default 180. Capped at 3600 (1h) — smoke should not run longer than a human attention span.

detail.description: Single-line reason string. Empty string is the convention when category=PASS. Free-form; typically the subprocess stderr tail or a one-line summary.

annotations.description: Open extension point for recon fields, tool-specific metadata, and forward-compatible annotations. Reserved for future use per the OpenAPI x-* / Kubernetes annotations pattern.

detail.description: Single-line reason string. Empty string is the convention when category=PASS. Free-form; typically the subprocess stderr tail or a one-line summary.

annotations.description: Open extension point for recon fields, tool-specific metadata, and forward-compatible annotations. Reserved for future use per the OpenAPI x-* / Kubernetes annotations pattern.
Open extension point for recon fields, tool-specific metadata, and forward-compatible annotations. Reserved for future use per the OpenAPI x-* / Kubernetes annotations pattern.

source_citations.description: Specific evidence used to write the recipe.

license.description: SPDX identifier convention (e.g. 'MIT', 'Apache-2.0'). Optional in v0.1.1; required before external contribution lands (phase 19+).

maintainer.description: Optional in v0.1.1; required before external contribution lands (phase 19+). When present, `name` is required inside the object — url alone does not identify a maintainer.

annotations.description: Open extension point for recon fields, tool-specific metadata, and forward-compatible annotations. Reserved for future use per the OpenAPI x-* / Kubernetes annotations pattern.

v0_2.description: v0.2 branch — structural clone of v0_1 with apiVersion const bumped and two optional additive top-level properties (persistent, channels). persistent and channels are optional even under v0.2: a recipe MAY declare apiVersion=ap.recipe/v0.2 without either block (e.g. to opt in to future v0.2-only additions landing after this phase).

name.description: Short slug used to tag the built image (ap-recipe-<name>) and as the default needle for response_contains_name.

api_key_by_provider.description: v0.2 (Phase 22b D-21) — per-provider env-var override map. Keys are LLM provider IDs (anthropic, openrouter, openai, ...); values are the env-var name to inject. Used by openclaw to declare `anthropic: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` so the persistent /start handler's _resolve_api_key_var can pick the right env-var when the user-selected model uses the anthropic-direct provider. Verified verbatim in recipes/openclaw.yaml lines 36-39.
